digest.component_or_area: store compatibility record + fail-closed capability gate
propositions: Introduce a committed store-level compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) at a fixed path, written atomically, that a v1.0 binary reads before any mutating or externally-publishing operation.
The gate fails closed on an unknown format_version, an unrecognized required capability, or a corrupt/unreadable record, while treating an absent record as legacy version 0 pass-through.

Insert the gate at the single structural choke point inside lock.acquire() for all write-intent acquisitions, with explicit gates for fsck_recover_cli's raw-git path and the outbound Jira push call-site, and CI-enforced coverage that no un-gated write path exists.
Read-only/diagnostic commands (fsck diagnostic, show, list, search) stay available (exit 0) under an incompatible or corrupt record, while fsck repair mode is gated as a write intent.
An idempotent ensure unit opportunistically writes the record when absent at init/fsck-repair/MCP-boot, and a missed fail-silent ensure sweep never blocks writes.

Rollback is safe because a pre-v1.0 binary ignores the new .store-compat.json file, proven by a hard test that runs fsck against a store carrying the record with the gate code absent and asserts operability and byte-unchanged file.
